dsl_rule.translation_notes: Interpreted 'recent swing high/low' using Donchian channels (20-period default) as a dynamic alternative to fixed historical boundaries. Volume confirmation uses hvol_20. Moving average logic uses sma_20 vs sma_50 crossovers. Exit uses support/resistance (s1/r1) to approximate 'close below 1.0x ATR from recent swing low' concept. Price structure reversals (2 consecutive lower lows) are approximated by MA crossunder combined with support break. Parameters trend_confirmation_bars=3 and structure_reversal_atr_multiple=1.0 inform the logic but are implementation details.

backtest_rule.reason: Interpreted 'recent swing high/low' using Donchian channels (20-period default) as a dynamic alternative to fixed historical boundaries. Volume confirmation uses hvol_20. Moving average logic uses sma_20 vs sma_50 crossovers. Exit uses support/resistance (s1/r1) to approximate 'close below 1.0x ATR from recent swing low' concept. Price structure reversals (2 consecutive lower lows) are approximated by MA crossunder combined with support break. Parameters trend_confirmation_bars=3 and structure_reversal_atr_multiple=1.0 inform the logic but are implementation details.

source_rule_text: A trader should use trend-following system logic to identify when a trend remains valid rather than imposing fixed historical boundaries on price movement, because markets can exceed historical extremes.
